question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Collections.singletonList(topic) passed to r.receive() in subscribe() to ensure that only one topic is subscribed per Flux, then open parallel subscriptions to multiple staking reward topics simultaneously, aggregating reward settlement data across all staking accounts to identify the largest unpaid rewards for targeted theft?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getSharedListener()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getSharedListener() does not check whether the requested topicId corresponds to a real topic in the database, allowing subscription to phantom topic IDs that may be used internally for staking reward calculation signaling, intercepting those signals?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the doOnSubscribe() logging in subscribe() which logs 'Creating shared subscription to {}' with the topic name, causing log injection by crafting a topic ID that produces a log entry containing malicious content that manipulates log aggregation systems monitoring staking reward activity?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: listen()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the publishOn(Schedulers.boundedElastic()) in SharedTopicListener.listen() to cause thread starvation by holding bounded elastic threads with slow consumers on staking reward topics, preventing other subscribers from receiving reward settlement messages in time to claim their unpaid rewards?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a replay(1) or cache() on the shared Flux in subscribe() to ensure that any subscriber joining after a staking reward settlement message is published will never receive that message, effectively hiding the reward settlement from late-joining legitimate subscribers?
